docs.text: ShowControl Manual Style Guide ¶ This guide defines how to write and maintain user documentation for ShowControl in a live-performance context. Scope: - Hardware controller - Desktop application - Ableton/script dependency - Optional Ableset integration 1) Audience model ¶ Every page must be written for one primary audience: Performer : fast, minimal, action-first Operator/Tech : precise, diagnostic, system-level If both are needed, split content into separate sections or pages. 2) Page structure rules ¶ Preferred section order: Goal Preconditions Steps Expected result Failure path (link to troubleshooting) For reference pages, use: Purpose Inputs/Outputs Limits/Defaults Operational notes 3) Responsibility boundaries (mandatory) ¶ When documenting a feature, explicitly state ownership: Hardware ShowControl app Ableton/script Ableset Avoid vague phrasing like “the system does X” without naming the layer. 4) Terminology (strict) ¶ Use these canonical terms only: Song : setlist song entry Section : section inside a Song User page : one configurable control page Scenes/Markers source : left source list in Setlist Do not alternate with synonyms inside docs. 5) Writing style ¶ Target style: technical/operator oriented . concise, direct, imperative no marketing language no ambiguous words (“maybe”, “probably”) in procedures short paragraphs and dense signal Good: - “Set IP and port, then press Send.” Avoid: - “You might want to check network settings if needed.” 6) Formatting standards ¶ Use ## for major sections, ### for substeps Use checklists for pre-show/showday tasks Use compact tables for mapping/reference data Use horizontal rules ( --- ) between major blocks Keep one task per numbered step For in-app docs navigation: - subsection labels must match heading text exactly where possible 7) Operational safety content ¶ Any live-critical page should include: What to verify before show What not to change mid-show Fallback sequence Recovery order If a section can break show flow, add an explicit warning note. 8) Troubleshooting design ¶ Troubleshooting must be symptom-first: “Controller not detected” “No Scenes/Markers shown” “Wrong Song/Section triggered” Each symptom entry should include: Fast checks (<= 60s) Deep checks Escalation/restart order 9) Versioning and change control ¶ For every doc update: keep terminology stable update submenu anchors if headings changed verify links in Info tab navigation commit with explicit scope ( docs(...) ) When large rewrites happen: - keep a dated backup snapshot of prior docs 10) Quality checklist before publish ¶ [ ] Audience is clear [ ] Preconditions are explicit [ ] Steps are executable as written [ ] Expected results are measurable [ ] Failure path is present [ ] Terminology is consistent [ ] Submenu anchors match headings [ ] No stale defaults/values 11) Recommended page set ¶ Minimum operational doc set: index.md (entry + scope) quickstart.md (performer path) showday-checklist.md (operator checklist) application.md (UI workflows) hardware.md (physical + IO) ableset.md (integration patterns) troubleshooting.md (symptom-first) STYLEGUIDE.md (this document)

docs.text: Using Ableset ¶ Purpose ¶ Define how ShowControl is operated when Ableset participates in the control/playback chain. Responsibility split ¶ ShowControl hardware : physical control surface input ShowControl app : mappings, setlist UI, operational state Ableton/script : musical timing and source context Ableset : orchestration and external show logic Operating patterns ¶ Pattern Primary logic owner Notes Ableton-first Ableton locators/markers Use Scenes/Markers source in Setlist tab Ableset-first Ableset orchestration Keep ShowControl as physical operator layer Preconditions ¶ Network topology known and stable Correct app/script instances loaded Setlist ownership decided (Ableton-first vs Ableset-first) Validation sequence ¶ Confirm connection path (USB/ETH). Confirm Song order and Section navigation. Validate Shift-layer controls. Validate deterministic jump behavior. Validate fallback behavior for path loss. Expected result - Operator can move Song/Section state predictably under load. If not, go to Troubleshooting .
docs.title: Using Ableset

docs.text: Desktop Application ¶ The ShowControl desktop app has five main tabs: Info Setlist Performance User Settings The typical workflow is: Configure device/network in Settings Build and verify content in Setlist Validate mappings in User Use Performance during rehearsal/show Main navigation ¶ The top bar is your global control area: main tab switching USB status indicator Ethernet status indicator What to verify first ¶ at least one active connection path (USB or ETH) expected tab selected for your current task Setlist tab ¶ Setlist is the primary preparation workspace. Main areas ¶ Left column : source content (Scenes/Markers) Right column : Songs/Sections list Top controls : undo/redo setlist selector new/rename/duplicate/delete setlist total setlist duration Typical setlist workflow ¶ Choose source ( Scenes or Markers ) Create or select target setlist Drag items into Songs Organize Sections within each Song Reorder for final show flow Validate duration and transitions Good practice ¶ keep Song names performance-friendly keep Sections meaningful for jumps/loops use undo/redo often during arrangement Performance tab ¶ Performance is a focused live-use view. Use it for fast access during rehearsal and show operation when you no longer need deep editing. User tab ¶ User tab defines controller behavior for each User page . You can configure ¶ button actions (normal + shifted) encoder turn/push actions pedal behavior display line content Recommended validation pass ¶ For each User page used in your show: Test critical transport actions Test next/previous Song behavior Test shifted actions Test pedals and encoder Settings tab ¶ Settings combines visual hardware settings and network communication settings. Device settings ¶ LED brightness display brightness Network settings ¶ IP address (4 fields) port send/apply action Notes ¶ keep production network values documented avoid changing network values during a live section Info tab ¶ Info is the built-in documentation viewer. left column: manual navigation right pane: current page content version label: quick app version reference Best-practice flow before a show ¶ Confirm USB/ETH status Confirm Settings values Finalize setlist order and durations Validate User page mappings Rehearse key transitions in Performance

docs.text: Installing and Connecting the ShowControl Controller ¶ Software Installation ¶ Ableton Live Control Surface Script ¶ Unzip the script archive. Copy the folder into Ableton Live’s Remote Scripts directory (usually found in the installation folder or your user library). Restart Ableton Live to detect the new script. ShowControl Application ¶ Download the .dmg file for the ShowControl app. Open the file and follow the instructions to install the application on your computer. Launch the app after installation. Hardware Connection ¶ USB Connection ¶ Connect the USB cable between the controller and your computer. Open Ableton Live, go to Preferences, and add a Control Surface line: select “Show Control” and set the MIDI In and Out ports to “Show Control DAW”. The controller will display a white SHCTRL icon and the number 1, indicating an active USB connection. Required - ShowControl Control Surface script running in Live, with In and Out attached to Show Control DAW port Ethernet Connection ¶ Connect the Ethernet cable between the controller and your network or directly to your computer. You can power the controller either by connecting the USB-C port to a charger or computer, or by using a Power over Ethernet (PoE) injector or switch on your Ethernet cable. On the controller, press and hold the encoder to access the Settings menu. Set the IP address according to your network requirements (make sure the first three octets match your computer’s address). On your computer, set the Ethernet port’s IPv4 address manually, using an address in the same subnet as the controller. Launch the ShowControl app: it will automatically detect your device and establish the connection. The SHCTRL icon on the controller will turn green and the number 1 will appear, indicating an active network connection. Make sure the Show Control script is loaded in Ableton Live so the app can communicate with Live. Required - ShowControl Control Surface script running in Live - ShowControl application running If Installation or Connection Fails ¶ See the Troubleshooting section for detailed solutions. Requirements - ShowControl controller and computer available - ShowControl app installed and working - Ableton Live project and script available (if using Ableton integration) - Required cables (USB-C, Ethernet, power)
docs.title: Installation / connection
Installing and Connecting the ShowControl Controller

docs.text: Unzip the script archive. Copy the folder into Ableton Live’s Remote Scripts directory (usually found in the installation folder or your user library). Restart Ableton Live to detect the new script.

docs.text: Connect the USB cable between the controller and your computer. Open Ableton Live, go to Preferences, and add a Control Surface line: select “Show Control” and set the MIDI In and Out ports to “Show Control DAW”. The controller will display a white SHCTRL icon and the number 1, indicating an active USB connection. Required - ShowControl Control Surface script running in Live, with In and Out attached to Show Control DAW port
docs.title: USB Connection

docs.text: Connect the Ethernet cable between the controller and your network or directly to your computer. You can power the controller either by connecting the USB-C port to a charger or computer, or by using a Power over Ethernet (PoE) injector or switch on your Ethernet cable. On the controller, press and hold the encoder to access the Settings menu. Set the IP address according to your network requirements (make sure the first three octets match your computer’s address). On your computer, set the Ethernet port’s IPv4 address manually, using an address in the same subnet as the controller. Launch the ShowControl app: it will automatically detect your device and establish the connection. The SHCTRL icon on the controller will turn green and the number 1 will appear, indicating an active network connection. Make sure the Show Control script is loaded in Ableton Live so the app can communicate with Live. Required - ShowControl Control Surface script running in Live - ShowControl application running
docs.title: Ethernet Connection

docs.text: See the Troubleshooting section for detailed solutions. Requirements - ShowControl controller and computer available - ShowControl app installed and working - Ableton Live project and script available (if using Ableton integration) - Required cables (USB-C, Ethernet, power)
docs.title: If Installation or Connection Fails

docs.text: Hardware Controller ¶ This page covers physical setup, on-stage usage, and quick diagnostics. Power ¶ Controller power options: USB-C PoE (if available on your hardware path) If both are connected, PoE may be prioritized depending on your hardware implementation. Recommendations ¶ use stable, tested power supplies avoid mid-show power path changes Connection modes ¶ Service Connection ShowControl App Ableton Script Ableset ShowControl USB Optional Recommended Optional ShowControl Ethernet Recommended Recommended Optional Ableset workflow USB Recommended Optional Required Ableset workflow Ethernet Optional Optional Required MIDI ports ¶ When connected by USB, expected ports are: Show Control DAW Show Control External MIDI If these are missing, check cable/port and reconnect sequence. Navigation ¶ Long encoder press : open menu/navigation Short encoder press : validate/select Shift/Lock : alternate actions / exit actions Setlist / Ableset page ¶ Used for Song and Section navigation in live context. Typical operations: previous/next Song section changes transport-oriented actions (depending on mapping/service mode) User pages ¶ Up to 4 User pages are available for custom control layouts. Each User page can combine: primary + shifted button actions encoder mappings pedal mappings display lines Settings page ¶ Hardware-side settings usually include: LED brightness display brightness network IP/port device ID Save values before exiting when applicable. Pedals ¶ Supported pedal modes: footswitch expression dual Verify pedal mode and wiring before show day. External MIDI ¶ External USB MIDI devices can be connected and forwarded through the external MIDI path. Use powered hubs for higher current devices. Quick pre-show hardware check ¶ power stable expected MIDI ports visible network link valid (if ETH) pedals/encoder responding
docs.title: Hardware Controller

docs.text: Quick Start (Performer) ¶ Goal ¶ Bring the rig to a stable, show-ready state with validated control and navigation. Preconditions ¶ Controller connected (USB and/or Ethernet) ShowControl app running on operator machine If using Ableton integration: set is open and script is loaded 1) Connect and validate transport path ¶ Connect controller via USB or Ethernet. Open ShowControl app. Confirm at least one active link indicator (USB or ETH). Expected result - Controller is reachable and status path is visible. If not, go to Troubleshooting . 2) Validate device/network settings ¶ Open Settings tab. Set LED/display brightness for venue conditions. If using Ethernet: verify IP + port, then press Send . Expected result - Brightness and network values apply without errors. If not, go to Troubleshooting . 3) Build or verify setlist structure ¶ Open Setlist tab. Select source ( Scenes or Markers ). Create/select setlist. Arrange Songs and Sections. Confirm order and transitions. Expected result - Setlist matches show order and is navigation-safe. If not, go to Troubleshooting . 4) Validate control mappings ¶ Open User tab. Validate critical actions per active User page: transport actions previous/next Song Shift layer encoder and pedals Expected result - Critical actions are deterministic and repeatable. If not, go to Troubleshooting . 5) Final pre-show gate ¶ [ ] Correct setlist selected [ ] First Song selected [ ] Shift state confirmed [ ] Connection path stable [ ] Critical controls re-tested If any item fails, stop and resolve before starting playback.
docs.title: Quick Start (Performer)

docs.text: Show Day Checklist ¶ Operator-first checklist for rehearsal and live runtime. Pre-show (T-30 to T-10) ¶ [ ] Controller powered and physically secured [ ] USB/ETH route validated [ ] App running on correct machine/profile [ ] Correct setlist loaded [ ] First Song selected [ ] Critical mappings validated (transport, Song nav, Shift) Line-check (T-10 to T-2) ¶ [ ] Play/Stop test [ ] Previous/Next Song test [ ] Section jump test [ ] Shift-layer test [ ] Pedal + encoder test During show (operator rules) ¶ Do not change IP/port mid-song. Do not remap controls mid-song unless emergency. Keep fallback path ready before each transition. Fallback protocol ¶ Verify active transport path (USB/ETH indicator). Verify selected setlist and current Song. Execute known-safe action (e.g., Play/Stop check). If unstable, restart app at safe transition point. Re-validate first actionable control before resuming. Post-show capture ¶ What failed? What took too long? Which action needs a dedicated control? Which mapping should be simplified?
docs.title: Show Day Checklist

docs.text: Operator-first checklist for rehearsal and live runtime.

docs.title: Line-check (T-10 to T-2)

docs.title: During show (operator rules)

docs.title: Fallback protocol

docs.title: Post-show capture

docs.text: Troubleshooting ¶ Symptom-first diagnostics for operator use. 1) Controller not detected ¶ Fast checks ¶ Use a data USB cable. Try another USB port. Restart controller + app. Confirm expected MIDI ports appear. Deep checks ¶ isolate to minimal rig (controller + single cable + app) check for competing MIDI clients Escalation ¶ reboot operator machine if port enumeration is inconsistent 2) No Scenes/Markers shown ¶ Fast checks ¶ Confirm Ableton set is open. Confirm remote script is loaded. Reconnect after Ableton is fully ready. Deep checks ¶ verify correct project context and expected markers/scenes exist verify app path is using intended source mode 3) Wrong Song/Section behavior ¶ Fast checks ¶ Verify Song order in Setlist tab. Verify Section placement inside each Song. Check for misplaced drops/duplicates. Use undo/redo to inspect latest operations. Deep checks ¶ validate jump logic against rehearsal sequence retest with simplified setlist slice 4) User page actions mismatch ¶ Fast checks ¶ Confirm active User page. Confirm Shift state. Re-check assignment values. Deep checks ¶ test one control at a time on isolated target action remove overlapping mappings during diagnosis 5) Network issues ¶ Fast checks ¶ Verify IP + port on both ends. Verify same subnet. Verify firewall policy. Deep checks ¶ test direct link test with reduced network load Recovery order (safe) ¶ Save state if possible Restart app Power cycle controller Re-validate first Song + critical controls Resume from known-safe transition point
docs.title: Troubleshooting

docs.text: Using Setlists ¶ Goal ¶ Create and operate a deterministic Song/Section structure for live performance. Preconditions ¶ Device connected and stable Source context available (Scenes/Markers or equivalent workflow) 1) Build setlist structure ¶ Open Setlist tab. Select source ( Scenes or Markers ). Create/select target setlist. Drag content into Songs . Organize Sections inside Songs. Verify ¶ Song order matches show order Section boundaries are meaningful for jumps/loops 2) Refine and validate ¶ Reorder Songs/Sections as needed. Use undo/redo while editing. Review durations and transition points. Verify ¶ no duplicate/misplaced Song or Section entries navigation is repeatable in rehearsal 3) Live operation guidance ¶ During show operation: avoid structural edits mid-song keep transition actions deterministic use known-safe jump points If setlist behavior is wrong ¶ Go to Troubleshooting .
docs.title: Using setlists

docs.text: Using User-Defined Controls ¶ Goal ¶ Configure and validate User pages so critical live actions are reliable under pressure. Preconditions ¶ Device connected Setlist baseline already validated 1) Configure User pages ¶ For each active User page : map primary button actions map shifted actions map encoder turn/push map pedal behavior set display lines if needed 2) Validation protocol ¶ For each User page, test in this order: Transport-critical actions Song navigation actions Section-related actions Shift-layer actions Encoder and pedal actions Verify ¶ each action triggers intended function only no overlapping/conflicting mappings shifted layer behaves consistently 3) Performance safety rules ¶ do not remap critical controls during active playback unless emergency prefer simplified mappings for show-critical actions keep one known-safe recovery action per User page If control behavior mismatches ¶ Go to Troubleshooting .
docs.title: Using user-defined controls

docs.text: Configure and validate User pages so critical live actions are reliable under pressure.
docs.title: Goal

docs.text: Device connected Setlist baseline already validated
docs.title: Preconditions

docs.text: For each active User page : map primary button actions map shifted actions map encoder turn/push map pedal behavior set display lines if needed
docs.title: 1) Configure User pages

docs.text: For each User page, test in this order: Transport-critical actions Song navigation actions Section-related actions Shift-layer actions Encoder and pedal actions
docs.title: 2) Validation protocol

docs.text: each action triggers intended function only no overlapping/conflicting mappings shifted layer behaves consistently
docs.title: Verify

docs.text: do not remap critical controls during active playback unless emergency prefer simplified mappings for show-critical actions keep one known-safe recovery action per User page
docs.title: 3) Performance safety rules

docs.text: Go to Troubleshooting .
docs.title: If control behavior mismatches
